api.purpose: Public API surface for OpenMeter: TypeSpec source (api/spec/) is the single source of truth that compiles to OpenAPI YAMLs, Go server stubs (api/api.gen.go, api/v3/api.gen.go), Go/JS/Python SDKs (api/client/). All downstream artefacts are generated — never edit them directly.

patterns.name: Two-step regeneration
patterns.description: TypeSpec changes require `make gen-api` (regenerates OpenAPI + SDKs) then `make generate` (regenerates Go server stubs, wire, ent). Both steps must run together.

patterns.name: Generated-file guard
patterns.description: Files with 'Code generated by X, DO NOT EDIT.' headers (api/api.gen.go, api/v3/api.gen.go, api/client/go/client.gen.go) must never be hand-edited.
patterns.example: // Code generated by github.com/oapi-codegen/oapi-codegen/v2 ... DO NOT EDIT.

patterns.name: v1 vs v3 package split
patterns.description: v1 API belongs in api/spec/packages/legacy/ and api/api.gen.go; v3 AIP API belongs in api/spec/packages/aip/ and api/v3/. Never mix content between versions.

patterns.name: api/types for manual gap-fill types
patterns.description: Only types that oapi-codegen cannot generate (oneOf/anyOf schemas) live in api/types via x-go-type references. No business logic or helpers go here.

patterns.name: Error responses via apierrors
patterns.description: All v3 error responses must go through the apierrors package for RFC 7807 compliance; direct http.Error or w.WriteHeader calls are forbidden.

key_file_guides.role: Generated v1 server stubs, enums, request/response types from openapi.yaml via oapi-codegen
key_file_guides.watch_for: DO NOT EDIT — regenerate via make gen-api

key_file_guides.role: Generated v3 server stubs from api/v3/openapi.yaml via oapi-codegen with chi-server + nullable types
key_file_guides.watch_for: DO NOT EDIT — regenerate via make gen-api

key_file_guides.role: oapi-codegen config for v3: nullable-type=true, always-prefix-enum-values, deepObject filter patch
key_file_guides.watch_for: custom chi-middleware.tmpl patch must stay aligned with codegen.yaml settings

key_file_guides.role: v1 TypeSpec entry point; all new legacy sub-domain .tsp files must be registered here and in cloud/main.tsp
key_file_guides.watch_for: Missing registration = endpoint not generated

key_file_guides.role: v3 AIP TypeSpec entry point; route/tag bindings are declared here only
key_file_guides.watch_for: @route and @tag must NOT be declared inside domain sub-folder operation files

key_file_guides.role: Generated Go SDK from api/openapi.cloud.yaml (not openapi.yaml)
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must not import internal monorepo packages — stays externally importable

key_file_guides.role: Generated v1 OpenAPI spec
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never hand-edit; always regenerate via make gen-api

anti_patterns: Hand-editing api/openapi.yaml, api/openapi.cloud.yaml, api/v3/openapi.yaml, or any *.gen.go file — always regenerate
Adding @route or @tag inside domain sub-folder TypeSpec files — routing belongs in root namespace files only
Adding business logic inside api/v3/ outside handlers/ sub-packages — api/v3 is infrastructure only
Adding new API types to client wrapper files instead of api/spec/ TypeSpec
Adding new legacy sub-domain TypeSpec file without registering in both main.tsp and cloud/main.tsp

decisions.decision: TypeSpec as single source of truth compiling to OpenAPI + multi-language SDKs
decisions.rationale: One spec feeds Go, JS, and Python SDKs plus two server versions — eliminates cross-language drift

decisions.decision: Go SDK generated from api/openapi.cloud.yaml, not openapi.yaml
decisions.rationale: Cloud variant exposes additional cloud-only endpoints not present in the self-hosted spec

decisions.decision: oapi-codegen with nullable-type:true and always-prefix-enum-values for v3
decisions.rationale: Correct null vs absent distinction for optional JSON fields; prefixed enums prevent name collisions

code_examples.scenario: Add a v3 API endpoint: author TypeSpec then implement handler

openmeter.purpose: Core business logic monorepo root: organises all domain packages (billing, customer, entitlement, subscription, meter, ingest, sink, notification, ledger, etc.) each following a strict service/adapter/httpdriver layering. No direct source files live here — all code is in sub-packages. The architectural constraint is that domain packages must not import app/common or cmd/* to remain independently testable and wirable.

patterns.name: Layered service/adapter split per domain
patterns.description: Every domain exposes a Service interface (openmeter/<domain>/service.go) implemented in <domain>/service/, an Adapter interface implemented in <domain>/adapter/, and HTTP handlers in <domain>/httpdriver/ or <domain>/httphandler/. Business logic lives in service/, persistence in adapter/, routing in httpdriver/.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo for all Ent adapter writes
patterns.description: Every Ent adapter method body wraps DB access with entutils.TransactingRepo / TransactingRepoWithNoValue so the ctx-bound Ent transaction is honored. Raw *entdb.Client helpers must also wrap.

patterns.name: Input.Validate() at every service boundary
patterns.description: All input types crossing a service boundary implement Validate() and are called before any business logic. Validation errors are returned as models.Generic* types or ValidationIssue sentinels.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: ServiceHooks / RequestValidatorRegistry for cross-domain callbacks
patterns.description: Cross-domain lifecycle reactions (billing hooking customer lifecycle, ledger hooking customer creation) must register via ServiceHooks or RequestValidatorRegistry — never via direct import of the other domain's service.

patterns.name: credits.enabled guarded at multiple independent layers
patterns.description: When credits.enabled=false, app/common must wire noop ledger implementations AND customer ledger hooks AND v3 credit handlers AND namespace default-account provisioning must all independently skip — no single guard suffices.

patterns.name: Domain test helpers isolated from app/common
patterns.description: Test fixtures under openmeter/<domain>/testutils/ must build deps from package constructors, never importing app/common — avoids import cycles.

patterns.name: clock.Now() everywhere, never time.Now()
patterns.description: All production code must call clock.Now() so tests can use FreezeTime/UnFreeze for deterministic time-dependent billing calculations.

key_file_guides.role: Composite billing.Service interface contract — no business logic here
key_file_guides.watch_for: Add methods to the interface; implementation goes in billing/service/

key_file_guides.role: Single codegen driver for all Ent-generated code in openmeter/ent/db/
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never edit openmeter/ent/db/ — it is fully generated by make generate

key_file_guides.role: Single Publisher facade routing events to ingest/system/balance-worker Kafka topics by event-name prefix
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always publish via eventbus.Publisher, never to raw topic strings

key_file_guides.role: RequestValidatorRegistry for pre-mutation cross-domain validation guards
key_file_guides.watch_for: Billing validators register here to enforce pre-condition checks without a circular import

key_file_guides.role: Manager fans out CreateNamespace/DeleteNamespace to all registered Handlers
key_file_guides.watch_for: Register namespace Handlers BEFORE CreateDefaultNamespace is called at startup

key_file_guides.role: Kafka→ClickHouse sink worker with exactly-once flush ordering
key_file_guides.watch_for: Flush order must be: ClickHouse insert → Kafka offset commit → Redis dedupe

key_file_guides.role: Zero-value noop implementations of all ledger interfaces — used when credits.enabled=false
key_file_guides.watch_for: Wire noop here when credits disabled; do NOT use a nil interface

key_file_guides.role: Shared Postgres test database provisioning via pgtestdb — skips when POSTGRES_HOST unset
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must never import app/common

anti_patterns: Importing app/common from any openmeter/<domain>/ or openmeter/testutils/ package — creates import cycles
Using time.Now() instead of clock.Now() in production code — breaks test time freezing
Adding business logic or DB queries directly to service.go or adapter.go interface files — logic belongs in service/ and adapter/ sub-packages
Calling sub-type connectors (metered entitlement, charge adapter) directly from HTTP handlers — always go through the top-level Service interface
Registering ledger namespace handlers or customer ledger hooks when credits.enabled=false — must use noop implementations

decisions.decision: Domain packages have no dependency on cmd/* or app/common
decisions.rationale: Keeps domain packages independently testable and prevents wire graph complexity from leaking into business logic

decisions.decision: ServiceHook / RequestValidatorRegistry pattern for cross-domain callbacks
decisions.rationale: Avoids circular imports between billing, customer, ledger, and entitlement while still allowing lifecycle reactions

decisions.decision: credits.enabled guarded at four independent wiring layers rather than one central check
decisions.rationale: Credits touches HTTP handlers, customer hooks, namespace provisioning, and charge creation — no single choke point controls all write paths

code_examples.scenario: New domain adapter method with transaction awareness

pkg.purpose: Shared infrastructure and utility layer for the entire monorepo: provides generic HTTP transport (pkg/framework/httptransport), Ent transaction management (pkg/framework/entutils), domain primitives (pkg/models), time/billing utilities (pkg/timeutil, pkg/datetime), Kafka plumbing (pkg/kafka), pagination (pkg/pagination), filters (pkg/filter), and dozens of small focused packages. Primary constraint: pkg/ must not import openmeter/* domain packages — it is a leaf dependency used by all domains.

patterns.name: pkg/framework as the universal infrastructure layer
patterns.description: All domain adapter code depends on pkg/framework/entutils for TransactingRepo, pkg/framework/httptransport for HTTP handler pipeline, pkg/framework/lockr for advisory locks, and pkg/framework/commonhttp for RFC 7807 error encoding. Never bypass these abstractions.

patterns.name: pkg/models as the foundational domain primitive — zero openmeter/* imports
patterns.description: pkg/models provides ValidationIssue, NamespacedID, ServiceHookRegistry, RFC 7807 Problem, etc. It must import nothing from openmeter/* — violations create circular deps that break the entire type system.

patterns.name: clock.Now() everywhere in production code
patterns.description: All time reads in production code must use pkg/clock.Now() not time.Now(). Tests use clock.FreezeTime / clock.UnFreeze for deterministic billing period calculations.

patterns.name: Typed errors via models.Generic* sentinels
patterns.description: Domain service and adapter methods must return typed errors (models.GenericNotFoundError, models.GenericConflictError, etc.) not raw fmt.Errorf — the HTTP error encoder maps these to correct status codes.

patterns.name: pkg/datetime for all calendar arithmetic
patterns.description: Month/year duration arithmetic must use pkg/datetime.DateTime.Add(ISODuration) not time.Duration arithmetic — month lengths vary and naive Duration math produces wrong billing period boundaries.

patterns.name: pkg/filter dual-output filter types
patterns.description: Query filters must implement both Ent selector predicates and go-sqlbuilder WHERE expressions via the pkg/filter.Filter interface. Setting multiple operator fields on one node is invalid — use Validate() to catch this early.

key_file_guides.role: TransactingRepo / TransactingRepoWithNoValue — read/write ctx-bound Ent transaction; central to all adapter DB access
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never call creator.Tx() or store *entdb.Tx on adapter structs — use TransactingRepo instead

key_file_guides.role: Generic Handler[Request,Response] decode/operate/encode pipeline with ErrorEncoder chain
key_file_guides.watch_for: Chain adds middleware; AppendOptions adds encoders — do not replace h.options

key_file_guides.role: pg_advisory_xact_lock distributed lock — requires active Postgres transaction in ctx
key_file_guides.watch_for: Locker panics if no tx in ctx; use SessionLocker for connection-scoped locks

key_file_guides.role: ValidationIssue immutable value type with With* copy-on-write builder
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never mutate a ValidationIssue in place — always use With* methods

key_file_guides.role: Generic ServiceHookRegistry for cross-domain lifecycle callbacks with re-entrant loop prevention
key_file_guides.watch_for: Loop-prevention uses pointer-identity context key — do not copy the registry

key_file_guides.role: Anchor-based billing period recurrence — not epoch-based
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always anchor to a truncated time; sub-millisecond drift produces off-by-nanosecond period boundaries

key_file_guides.role: Typed Kafka config structs producing kafka.ConfigMap with validation
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never set kafka.ConfigMap keys directly — bypasses validation and localhost IPv4 auto-fix

key_file_guides.role: Page/Result[T] contract for all domain List methods; Result.MarshalJSON flattens page fields
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use MapResult/MapResultErr — manual Result construction misses TotalCount and Page echo

anti_patterns: Importing openmeter/* domain packages from any pkg/ sub-package — creates circular dependencies; pkg/ is a leaf
Using time.Now() instead of clock.Now() in any pkg/ or openmeter/ production code
Returning plain fmt.Errorf from service/adapter code where models.Generic* typed errors are expected — HTTP encoder falls through to 500
Using time.Duration arithmetic for month/year periods instead of pkg/datetime ISODuration arithmetic
Calling entutils.TransactingRepo with a raw *entdb.Client in a helper that is called inside a transaction — must still wrap so rebinding honors the ctx tx

decisions.decision: pkg/ has zero imports from openmeter/* domain packages
decisions.rationale: Prevents circular dependency; all seven domain binaries and their test suites depend on pkg/ — any openmeter/* import would create a cycle

decisions.decision: pkg/framework/entutils.TransactingRepo reads transaction from ctx rather than accepting *entdb.Tx
decisions.rationale: Ent transactions propagate implicitly via ctx; parameter-passing would leak tx plumbing into every adapter call site

decisions.decision: pkg/models.ValidationIssue uses private constructor and With* copy-on-write pattern
decisions.rationale: Immutability prevents accidental mutation as issues propagate through service → adapter → HTTP encoder chain

code_examples.scenario: HTTP handler using the httptransport pipeline with error encoding
